message:
fix(bash): remove double quotes from array keys in errors.sh and onboard.sh

Continued fix for set -u compatibility. Found additional files with
double-quoted associative array keys that could trigger "unbound variable"
errors:

- scripts/lib/errors.sh: ERROR_PATTERNS array (27 entries)
  Changed ["key"] to ['key'] (single quotes preserve special chars in keys)

- packages/onboard/onboard.sh: AUTH_SERVICE_* arrays (3 arrays, 24 entries)
  Changed ["key"] to [key] (unquoted)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -74,36 +74,36 @@ declare -a AUTH_SERVICES=(

+    [tailscale]="Secure VPS access via private network"

+    [codex]="OpenAI's AI coding agent"
+    [gemini]="Google's AI coding agent"
+    [github]="Code hosting and version control"
+    [vercel]="Frontend deployment platform"
+    [supabase]="Database and auth backend"
+    [cloudflare]="CDN and edge computing"
 )
 
 declare -A AUTH_SERVICE_COMMANDS=(
-    ["tailscale"]="sudo tailscale up"
-    ["claude"]="claude"
-    ["codex"]="codex login"
-    ["gemini"]="gemini"
-    ["github"]="gh auth login"
-    ["vercel"]="vercel login"
-    ["supabase"]="supabase login"
-    ["cloudflare"]="wrangler login"
+    [tailscale]="sudo tailscale up"
+    [claude]="claude"
+    [codex]="codex login"
+    [gemini]="gemini"
+    [github]="gh auth login"
+    [vercel]="vercel login"
+    [supabase]="supabase login"
+    [cloudflare]="wrangler login"
 )
 
 # Colors (works in most terminals) - fallback if gum_ui not loaded

@@ -17,88 +17,89 @@ export ACFS_ERRORS_LOADED=1
 # ============================================================
 
 # Common installation errors and their fixes
-# Format: ["error pattern"]="suggested fix"
+# Format: [error pattern]="suggested fix"
+# Note: Array keys must NOT use double quotes with set -u (causes variable expansion)
 # shellcheck disable=SC2034  # Used by get_suggested_fix()
 declare -A ERROR_PATTERNS=(
     # Network connectivity issues
-    ["curl: (7) Failed to connect"]="Network connection failed. Check internet connectivity:
  curl -I https://google.com
Also verify firewall allows outbound HTTPS (port 443)."
+    ['curl: (7) Failed to connect']="Network connection failed. Check internet connectivity:
  curl -I https://google.com
Also verify firewall allows outbound HTTPS (port 443)."
 
-    ["curl: (6) Could not resolve"]="DNS resolution failed. Check DNS configuration:
  cat /etc/resolv.conf
  ping -c1 8.8.8.8
If ping works but DNS doesn't, try adding 'nameserver 8.8.8.8' to /etc/resolv.conf"
+    ['curl: (6) Could not resolve']="DNS resolution failed. Check DNS configuration:
  cat /etc/resolv.conf
  ping -c1 8.8.8.8
If ping works but DNS doesn't, try adding 'nameserver 8.8.8.8' to /etc/resolv.conf"
 
-    ["curl: (28) Connection timed out"]="Network timeout. This could be:
  1. Slow/unstable internet connection
  2. Firewall blocking outbound HTTPS
  3. Upstream server temporarily down
Try: curl -v https://google.com --connect-timeout 10"
+    ['curl: (28) Connection timed out']="Network timeout. This could be:
  1. Slow/unstable internet connection
  2. Firewall blocking outbound HTTPS
  3. Upstream server temporarily down
Try: curl -v https://google.com --connect-timeout 10"
 
-    ["curl: (35) SSL connect error"]="SSL/TLS handshake failed. Possible causes:
  1. Outdated ca-certificates: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates
  2. System clock wrong: date && sudo ntpdate -s time.nist.gov
  3. Corporate proxy/firewall intercepting HTTPS"
+    ['curl: (35) SSL connect error']="SSL/TLS handshake failed. Possible causes:
  1. Outdated ca-certificates: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates
  2. System clock wrong: date && sudo ntpdate -s time.nist.gov
  3. Corporate proxy/firewall intercepting HTTPS"
 
-    ["Connection refused"]="Connection refused by remote server. The service may be:
  1. Temporarily down - wait and retry
  2. Blocked by firewall - check outbound rules
  3. Rate limited - wait 60 seconds"
+    ['Connection refused']="Connection refused by remote server. The service may be:
  1. Temporarily down - wait and retry
  2. Blocked by firewall - check outbound rules
  3. Rate limited - wait 60 seconds"
 
     # APT/package management
-    ["E: Unable to locate package"]="Package not found in APT repositories. Try:
  sudo apt-get update
If still failing, the package may not exist for your Ubuntu version."
+    ['E: Unable to locate package']="Package not found in APT repositories. Try:
  sudo apt-get update
If still failing, the package may not exist for your Ubuntu version."
 
-    ["E: Could not get lock"]="APT lock held by another process. Solutions:
  1. Wait for other installs to finish
  2. Check: ps aux | grep -E 'apt|dpkg'
  3. If stuck: sudo rm /var/lib/apt/lists/lock (use with caution)"
+    ['E: Could not get lock']="APT lock held by another process. Solutions:
  1. Wait for other installs to finish
  2. Check: ps aux | grep -E 'apt|dpkg'
  3. If stuck: sudo rm /var/lib/apt/lists/lock (use with caution)"
 
-    ["dpkg: error processing"]="DPKG database corrupted. Try:
  sudo dpkg --configure -a
  sudo apt-get install -f"
+    ['dpkg: error processing']="DPKG database corrupted. Try:
  sudo dpkg --configure -a
  sudo apt-get install -f"
 
-    ["Unmet dependencies"]="Package dependencies cannot be satisfied. Try:
  sudo apt-get install -f
  sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade"
+    ['Unmet dependencies']="Package dependencies cannot be satisfied. Try:
  sudo apt-get install -f
  sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade"
 
-    ["Hash Sum mismatch"]="APT cache corrupted. Fix with:
  sudo rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  sudo apt-get update"
+    ['Hash Sum mismatch']="APT cache corrupted. Fix with:
  sudo rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  sudo apt-get update"
 
     # Permission issues
-    ["Permission denied"]="Permission issue. Ensure you're running with appropriate privileges:
  1. Run with sudo: sudo bash install.sh
  2. Or run as root user
  3. Check file permissions: ls -la"
+    ['Permission denied']="Permission issue. Ensure you're running with appropriate privileges:
  1. Run with sudo: sudo bash install.sh
  2. Or run as root user
  3. Check file permissions: ls -la"
 
-    ["Operation not permitted"]="Operation blocked. You may need:
  1. Root/sudo access
  2. To disable read-only filesystem protection
  3. To check SELinux/AppArmor restrictions"
+    ['Operation not permitted']="Operation blocked. You may need:
  1. Root/sudo access
  2. To disable read-only filesystem protection
  3. To check SELinux/AppArmor restrictions"
 
     # Disk space
-    ["No space left on device"]="Disk is full. Free up space:

  sudo apt-get clean  # Clear APT cache

+    ['No space left on device']="Disk is full. Free up space:

-    ["disk quota exceeded"]="Disk quota exceeded. Contact system administrator or:

  du -sh ~/* | sort -h  # Find large directories"
+    ['disk quota exceeded']="Disk quota exceeded. Contact system administrator or:
  quota -v  # Check your quota
  du -sh ~/* | sort -h  # Find large directories"
 
     # GPG/signing issues
-    ["gpg: keyserver receive failed"]="GPG keyserver unreachable. Alternatives:
  1. Retry later - keyservers are sometimes slow
  2. Try different keyserver: --keyserver hkp://keyserver.ubuntu.com:80
  3. Check firewall allows port 11371 or 80"
+    ['gpg: keyserver receive failed']="GPG keyserver unreachable. Alternatives:
  1. Retry later - keyservers are sometimes slow
  2. Try different keyserver: --keyserver hkp://keyserver.ubuntu.com:80
  3. Check firewall allows port 11371 or 80"
 
-    ["NO_PUBKEY"]="Missing GPG key for repository. Try:
  sudo apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys <KEY_ID>"
+    ['NO_PUBKEY']="Missing GPG key for repository. Try:
  sudo apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys <KEY_ID>"
 
     # Verification/checksum
-    ["checksum mismatch"]="Upstream installer script has changed. This could mean:
  1. Legitimate update - check the tool's GitHub for release notes
  2. Potential tampering - verify manually before proceeding
See: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/issues"

  1. Legitimate update - check the tool's GitHub for release notes
  2. Potential tampering - verify manually before proceeding
See: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/issues"
 
-    ["signature verification failed"]="GPG signature verification failed. The file may be:
  1. Corrupted during download - retry
  2. Tampered with - do not proceed
  3. Using outdated signing key"
+    ['signature verification failed']="GPG signature verification failed. The file may be:
  1. Corrupted during download - retry
  2. Tampered with - do not proceed
  3. Using outdated signing key"
 
     # Rate limiting
-    ["rate limit"]="API rate limit exceeded. Solutions:
  1. Wait 60 seconds and retry
  2. If using GitHub: authenticate with 'gh auth login'
  3. Consider using a different network/IP"
+    ['rate limit']="API rate limit exceeded. Solutions:
  1. Wait 60 seconds and retry
  2. If using GitHub: authenticate with 'gh auth login'
  3. Consider using a different network/IP"
 
-    ["Too Many Requests"]="Server rate limiting requests. Wait 1-5 minutes before retrying."
+    ['Too Many Requests']="Server rate limiting requests. Wait 1-5 minutes before retrying."
 
-    ["API rate limit exceeded"]="GitHub API rate limit. Authenticate to increase limit:
  gh auth login
Or wait ~60 minutes for limit reset."
+    ['API rate limit exceeded']="GitHub API rate limit. Authenticate to increase limit:
  gh auth login
Or wait ~60 minutes for limit reset."
 
     # Memory issues
-    ["Cannot allocate memory"]="System out of memory. Try:
  1. Close other applications
  2. Add swap: sudo fallocate -l 2G /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile
  3. Use a VPS with more RAM (4GB+ recommended)"
+    ['Cannot allocate memory']="System out of memory. Try:
  1. Close other applications
  2. Add swap: sudo fallocate -l 2G /swapfile && sudo mkswap /swapfile && sudo swapon /swapfile
  3. Use a VPS with more RAM (4GB+ recommended)"
 
-    ["Killed"]="Process killed (likely OOM killer). Your VPS may need more RAM.
  Minimum: 4GB recommended for ACFS install.
  Check: free -h"
+    ['Killed']="Process killed (likely OOM killer). Your VPS may need more RAM.
  Minimum: 4GB recommended for ACFS install.
  Check: free -h"
 
     # Git issues
-    ["fatal: not a git repository"]="Not in a git repository. Ensure you're in the correct directory:
  pwd
  git status"
+    ['fatal: not a git repository']="Not in a git repository. Ensure you're in the correct directory:
  pwd
  git status"
 
-    ["fatal: remote origin already exists"]="Git remote already configured. This is usually fine. To reset:
  git remote remove origin
  git remote add origin <url>"
+    ['fatal: remote origin already exists']="Git remote already configured. This is usually fine. To reset:
  git remote remove origin
  git remote add origin <url>"
 
     # Node/Bun issues
-    ["EACCES: permission denied"]="Node/Bun permission issue. Don't use sudo with npm/bun global installs.
  Instead, fix permissions: chown -R \$USER ~/.npm ~/.bun"
+    ['EACCES: permission denied']="Node/Bun permission issue. Don't use sudo with npm/bun global installs.
  Instead, fix permissions: chown -R \$USER ~/.npm ~/.bun"
 
-    ["ENOENT: no such file or directory"]="File or directory not found. Check:
  1. Current directory: pwd
  2. File exists: ls -la
  3. Path is correct"
+    ['ENOENT: no such file or directory']="File or directory not found. Check:
  1. Current directory: pwd
  2. File exists: ls -la
  3. Path is correct"
 
     # Python/uv issues
-    ["No module named"]="Python module not found. Install with:
  uv pip install <module>
  Or: pip install <module>"
+    ['No module named']="Python module not found. Install with:
  uv pip install <module>
  Or: pip install <module>"
 
     # Rust/cargo issues
     ["linker 'cc' not found"]="C compiler not installed. Install build tools:
  sudo apt-get install -y build-essential"
 
-    ["Could not compile"]="Rust compilation failed. Usually needs:
  sudo apt-get install -y build-essential pkg-config libssl-dev"
+    ['Could not compile']="Rust compilation failed. Usually needs:
  sudo apt-get install -y build-essential pkg-config libssl-dev"
 
     # Timeout/hanging
-    ["timed out"]="Operation timed out. This could be:
  1. Slow network - retry on stable connection
  2. Overloaded server - wait and retry
  3. Firewall blocking - check outbound rules"
+    ['timed out']="Operation timed out. This could be:
  1. Slow network - retry on stable connection
  2. Overloaded server - wait and retry
  3. Firewall blocking - check outbound rules"
 
     # SSH issues (for user guidance)
-    ["Connection closed by remote host"]="SSH connection closed. Possible causes:
  1. Server rebooted - wait 30s and reconnect
  2. Network interruption - check your connection
  3. Server-side issue - contact VPS provider if persists"
+    ['Connection closed by remote host']="SSH connection closed. Possible causes:
  1. Server rebooted - wait 30s and reconnect
  2. Network interruption - check your connection
  3. Server-side issue - contact VPS provider if persists"
 
-    ["Host key verification failed"]="SSH host key changed. If expected (new VPS), fix with:
  ssh-keygen -R <hostname>
If unexpected, verify you're connecting to the right server!"
+    ['Host key verification failed']="SSH host key changed. If expected (new VPS), fix with:
  ssh-keygen -R <hostname>
If unexpected, verify you're connecting to the right server!"
 )
